title: The New Tenant's Checklist for Managing Sub-Metered Electricity and Water Connections Post-Move
excerpt: Sub-metered electricity water checklist: new tenant post-move utilities, condo metering, prepaid tokens, water tank management Addis Ababa tenant guide.
content:
The new tenant's checklist for managing sub-metered electricity and water connections post-move

Modern condos often sub-meter electricity and water per unit—master building meter splits responsibility you never saw in old kebele flat rate. New tenant post-move checklist prevents first-week blackout, disputed bills, and angry management when your token empty silences elevator mid-unpack.

This guide manages sub-metered utilities after moving into Addis condominiums.

Electricity sub-meter checklist

  • Photograph meter reading day one before load—baseline timestamp.
  • Confirm prepaid versus postpaid—buy initial token stock.
  • Learn recharge app or vendor location compound gate.
  • Circuit breaker map label which feeds AC versus kitchen.
  • Report faulty meter management before dispute accumulates.

Water sub-meter or allocation

Some towers sub-meter water; others allocate by square meter plus tank schedule. Document first reading; understand tank fill hours; locate shutoff valve leak emergencies. Heater switch labeled—empty tank burn element disaster.

Management handover meeting

Ask written tariff structure; service charge what includes; generator fuel share calculation; penalty late payment. Save management WhatsApp maintenance.

Move day power needs

Ensure token credit before elevator and crew tools charge—negotiate pre-move top-up landlord sometimes.

Neighbor comparison sanity

Abnormal spike week one—leak hunt toilet running; AC left on empty unit previous tenant not your fault but fix before bill.

Documentation folder digital

Meter photos monthly; payment receipts; correspondence management disputes.

Active Movers coordination

Confirm power live elevator book same confirmation email utilities.

Conclusion

Sub-metered utilities post-move need day-one readings, token readiness, management clarity, and monthly photo habit—new tenant checklist prevents surprise dark apartment week one.
author: Active Movers Team
